headline: Deere & Co agrees to pay $99 million to settle 'right to repair' lawsuit
summary: Deere & Co. has agreed to pay $99 million as part of a settlement that would resolve a class action lawsuit accusing the farm equipment giant of monopolizing repair services.  The Moline, Illinois-based manufacturer, which does business under the John Deere brand, has faced a handful of “right to repair” complaints over the years.  The deal announced Monday — which still needs final approval from the court — would settle a 2022 lawsuit that accused the company of withholding repair software and conspiring with authorized dealers to force farmers to use their services for repairs, when they could otherwise fix tractors and other equipment themselves or use independent alternatives.

headline: DE Removes Legal Overhang by Settling 'Right to Repair' Litigation
summary: Deere settles 'right to repair' litigation with no wrongdoing found, creating a class fund while reinforcing its push for customer-focused repair solutions.

headline: Deere Pays Millions To Settle Repair Clash Without Admitting Fault
summary: Deere (NYSE: DE) reaches $99M settlement in "right to repair" litigation, ensuring farmer access to diagnostic tools for the next decade.

headline: Reported Earlier, Deere & Company Reaches Settlement In Right To Repair Antitrust Case, To Distribute Class Settlement Funds Pending US District Court Approval
summary: Reported Earlier, Deere & Company Reaches Settlement In Right To Repair Antitrust Case, To Distribute Class Settlement Funds Pending US District Court Approval

raw.headline: Reported Earlier, Deere & Company Reaches Settlement In Right To Repair Antitrust Case, To Distribute Class Settlement Funds Pending US District Court Approval

headline: Deere & Company Reaches Settlement in Repair Services Antitrust Litigation
summary: Deere & Company (NYSE: DE) announced today that it has reached a settlement agreement to resolve the multidistrict "right to repair" litigation pending in the United States District Court for the Northern District of Illinois. This settlement addresses the issues raised in the 2022 complaint and brings this case to an end with no finding of wrongdoing.
